title: The Starfish: A Giant Step Towards Origins of Life
author: Perez, Jean claude
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0

steps.instruction: ABSTRACT : Here we study the circular RNA sequence of 22 bases named ALpha, which is supposed to be a candidate for the emergence of life on earth. We demonstrate how this sequence is structured by Fibonacci proportions, then we demonstrate that, among the millions of known sequences, it is in the starphish genome that we find this sequence with the highest homology, thus uniting the scales of RNA and morphogenesis since the geometry of the starfish is also structured by the Golden Ratio.
